# Source Card: Creation and Evaluation of New Growth Charts With a Gradual Transition From WHO to CDC Values

## Summary
This peer-reviewed Pediatrics (AAP) retrospective cohort study (n=7429) shows that the CDC-recommended abrupt switch from the WHO Growth Standards to the CDC 2000 Growth Reference at exactly age 2 years introduces a chart-source artifact: a large fall in mean BMI-for-age and weight-for-age z-scores between 1.5 and 2 years. The authors construct alternative charts that blend WHO and CDC via a weighted average across ages 2-5 years, which nearly eliminates the artifact and reduces the proportion of children flagged with a large z-score drop. For the pediatric CDS module this bears on the age-2 growth-chart transition boundary and on how a "large" z-score change (<-1 / >1) is defined when flagging slow weight gain.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) CDC switches WHO->CDC 2000 at age 2 years — locator: Abstract Background — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:966c4c44cab42b2689c82651cd27a0917f472e9502897124625917d43d895331]"
- (ev_002) Large z-score change defined as <-1 (drop) or >1 (rise) — locator: Abstract Methods — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:b97768fbc16002be9946ef47685297a9cbb7b4455cbd7fcebcbf71b1f200c900]"
- (ev_003) Gradual weighted-average transition applied ages 2-5y — locator: Abstract Results — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:da6cdd97e62d1fe68c74596619d99b730c7718e908fea85a082bc165e477e6b4]"
- (ev_004) Abrupt-switch mean z drop (BMIz -0.59, WTz -0.35) vs gradual (BMIz -0.09, WTz -0.01), n=7429 — locator: Abstract Results — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:2451fc587b071a0a2356bc831bbc2c79bfc13f79c7db515c96784d867f4a9a9c]"
- (ev_005) Proportion with a large drop: CDC BMIz 28.3% / WTz 6.0% vs gradual 11.6% / 0.85% — locator: Abstract Results — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:a1d7ec7b4c2f0963e2385d82b8e36340a7f1038136db8531ab34be58db4ce012]"
- (ev_006) Gradual charts may reduce overidentification of slow weight gain — locator: Abstract Conclusions — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:b62db5417918d464bfe01ccfe93ca663b5b3a27832478601c2b4f39097d2b4b8]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: young US children (~1.5-5 years), both sexes, real-world retrospective cohort (n=7429); relevant to BMI-for-age, weight-for-age, and length/height-for-age. Not a laboratory-assay reference-interval source — thresholds are anthropometric z-scores and chart-source boundaries, so there is no analyzer/method dependence.
- Chart lifecycle: WHO Growth Standards apply 0-2y and CDC 2000 Growth Reference applies >=2y under current CDC recommendation. The study proposes a gradual-transition alternative blended over ages 2-5y (implementation_proposal, not yet a governing standard).

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- Indirect: the z-score-drop threshold (change <-1) functions as a slow-weight-gain / faltering-growth flag. The study's core finding is that applying this flag at the abrupt age-2 chart boundary causes overidentification (a false scope-exit / referral trigger). A CDS converter should treat the age-2 WHO->CDC switch as an artifact-prone boundary, not average the two chart values. No diagnosis, treatment, or referral directive is asserted here.

## Limitations & conflicts
- Full publisher text was WAF-blocked (HTTP 403) at access; all verbatim numbers were captured from the PubMed-indexed structured abstract (Pediatrics vol 156(3), e2025070697). Exact per-sex, per-age-month, or figure/table-level cutoff values beyond the abstract are NOT locatable in the retrieved material — do not assert them.
- Conflict preserved (not collapsed): the CDC-recommended abrupt WHO->CDC switch at 2y and the authors' proposed gradual 2-5y blend produce materially different z-scores at the same age; these are distinct chart-source conventions and must not be averaged into a single value in the CDS module. The gradual chart is an implementation_proposal, not an adopted CDC/WHO standard.
- This source establishes growth-chart methodology only; it defines no laboratory assay threshold, GA/PNA-banded reference interval, or hematology/chemistry cutoff.
